entry.title: Washington Post: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Milley equates ‘horrors’ in Ukraine with suffering during World War II
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Fighting in Severodonetsk has ‘worsened,’ official says; Britain pledges rocket systems
  • Deadly church attack rattles a peaceful corner of Nigeria
  • Who could replace Boris Johnson? Here are some of the contenders.
  • China secretly building PLA naval facility in Cambodia, Western officials say
  • How Boris Johnson went from landslide victory to no-confidence vote
  • Britain’s Boris Johnson to face no-confidence vote from his party
  • 49 killed and hundreds injured in Bangladesh container depot fire
  • U.S. and South Korea respond to North Korean launch with 8 missiles of their own
  • Putin threatens new targets if Ukraine gets longer-range rockets; missiles hit Kyiv
  • Millions of Ukrainian refugees may stay in E.U., top official says
  • Putin threatens to hit new targets if long-range missiles are sent to Ukraine
  • Queen Elizabeth II makes surprise appearance for final day of jubilee
  • The big lunch: Britain celebrates the queen’s jubilee with picnic spreads
  • Dominican Republic cabinet minister killed in office
  • Russian opera 'Boris Godunov' to open next La Scala season
  • Burglars cut through wall to rob bank deposit boxes in Iran
  • Nigerian forces hunt for gunmen who killed 50 at church
  • British journalist, Brazilian expert missing in the Amazon
  • NATO nations block Russian envoy's plane from Serbia visit
  • South Africa's president faces probe over unreported theft
  • South African pastor found guilty of treason, racist plot
  • In Iraq, British man gets 15 years over smuggling artifacts
  • Migrant caravan sets out in southern Mexico
  • There have been over 200 mass shootings so far in 2022
  • A mother’s desperate bid to save her anguished teen ends in gunfire
  • After shooting, Uvalde residents say need for mental health care is high
  • Anger and heartbreak on Bus No. 15
  • Man drowns as Arizona police watch: ‘I’m not jumping in after you’
  • Pete Buttigieg says blaming doorways for school shootings is ‘insanity’
  • Children’s museum apologizes for selling Juneteenth watermelon salad
  • ‘When it’s a shooting on a city street, nothing happens’
  • A liberal D.A. finds voters’ moods have changed even in San Francisco
  • Post Politics Now: Jan. 6, guns on the radar for Congress; Biden announces latest baby formula flight
  • Elon Musk threatens to back out of Twitter deal over withholding data
  • White House takes steps to spur solar industry
  • ‘We’re all afraid’: Massive rent increases hit mobile homes
  • Apple unveils new MacBooks, iOS 16 features and more
  • Common lease terms renters should know before signing on dotted line
  • Will a squabble between siblings over a home lead to an eviction?
  • Two-bedroom, two-bath condo in Reston lists at $399,000
  • Supreme Court’s Leak Investigation Is Self-Destructive
  • JetBlue’s New Takeover Offer Puts Rival Bidder Frontier in Hot Seat
  • Wordle, BeReal and Even Facebook: Apps Get Less Addictive
  • Doctors Are Still Flying Blind With Paxlovid
  • Here’s How US States Differ on Carrying Guns in Public
  • How China Is Reviving Tools for Hedging Credit Risk
  • ‘Fake’ Aluminum Stocks Put Perils of China’s Commodities Funding in Spotlight
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • Pride rainbows mean I am not alone
  • What the Arizona GOP’s response to Uvalde reveals
  • Why losing the midterms isn’t the worst thing for Democrats
  • Metro is short on trains, drivers — and public confidence
  • Most Americans don’t accept the mass slaughter of children. Why does the GOP?
  • On crime and the economy, Republicans dictate the media narrative
  • What Chesa Boudin’s recall vote could mean for criminal justice reform
  • The California primary reminds us why our politics are broken
  • No school should have to close because of extreme heat
  • Free speech can’t trump every other value on campus
  • Cartoon by Joe Heller
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • How Boris Johnson Suffered a Stunning Political Reversal
  • Boris Johnson Makes His Case in Speech to Conservatives: ‘We Can Unite’
  • Who Could Replace Boris Johnson as Conservative Party Leader?
  • Russia Seeks Buyers for Plundered Ukraine Grain, U.S. Warns
  • Battered by Russian Shells, a Monastery Remains Loyal to Moscow
  • Ukraine Insurgents Strike in Russia-Occupied Regions
  • With Cornyn in the Room, Senate Gun Talks Focus on Narrow Changes
  • Big City Mayors, Furious About Mass Shootings, Fear Sweeping Gun Limits Are Out of Reach
  • The Potential Dark Side of a White-Hot Labor Market
  • Covid at Home: Why Only Some People Test Positive
  • The latest virus wave shows signs of abating in the Northeast.
  • Beijing relaxes Covid measures as fears of another outbreak linger.
  • Elon Musk Threatens to End Twitter Deal Without Information on Spam Accounts
  • Outcry Over High School Clinic Exposes Deep Divisions on Mental Health
  • Behold the Lionfish, as Transfixing as It Is Destructive
  • There Has to Be a Tipping Point on Guns, Right?
  • Democrats Can Win This Fall if They Make One Key Promise
  • It’s Been 50 Years. I Am Not ‘Napalm Girl’ Anymore.

  • We Need to Stay Heartbroken About School Shootings
  • Students, We Want to Hear What You Have to Say About School Shootings

  • What Depp v. Heard Means for #MeToo
  • Parents of Premature Babies Struggle Amid Formula Shortage

  • Live Updates: Britain Offers Long-Range Weapons to Ukraine, as Russian Artillery Hammers East
  • Live Updates: Boris Johnson Faces No-Confidence Vote in U.K.

  • Queen’s Jubilee Celebrations Offers Britons Respite From Woes

  • U.S. and South Korea Launch Missiles In Response to North Korean Tests
  • Dozens Feared Dead in Church Attack in Owo, Nigeria

  • High-tech Western Weapons Pose Challenges for Ukraine Military
  • British pound and stocks rise as vote on prime minister washes over markets.
  • Tropical Storm Alex Takes Aim at Bermuda After Flooding Florida

  • Your Monday Briefing
  • Struggling in Ukraine’s East, Russian Forces Strike in Kyiv
  • Your Monday Briefing: Russian Missiles Hit Kyiv
  • Putin Ally Mines Gold and Plays Favorites in Sudan
  • Mass Shooting in Philadelphia Kills at Least 3 on Weekend of Gun Violence
  • In San Francisco, Democrats Are at War With Themselves Over Crime
  • Georgia Candidate Who Appeared to Get Few Votes Was Actually in 1st Place
  • A Pro-Herschel Walker Group Gave Out Gas Vouchers, Angering Democrats
  • Cargo Ship Crew Stuck in China Due to Covid Delays and Quarantine
  • High California Gas Prices Rattle Democrats Ahead of Midterms
  • A Broken Redistricting Process Winds Down, With No Repairs in Sight
  • Supreme Court May Hear ‘800-Pound Gorilla’ of Election Law Cases
  • In Races to Run Elections, Candidates Are Backed by Key 2020 Deniers
  • Thefts, Fraud and Lawsuits at the World’s Biggest NFT Marketplace
  • Biden to Pause New Solar Tariffs as White House Aims to Boost Adoption
  • Apple Unveils Software Updates for iOS 16
  • Punctuality Is Having a Moment
  • Mercedes-Benz Recalls Nearly 1 Million Vehicles Over Brake Issue
  • Biden v. Musk
  • Beijing Relaxes Covid Measures as Outbreak Fears Linger
  • Biden Has ‘Only Bad Options’ for Bringing Down Oil Prices
  • In Elderly Americans, Rising Debt May Adversely Affect Health
  • The Office Monsters Are Trying to Claw Their Way Back to 2019
  • Apple Sees Virtual-Reality Headset as Its Next Big Thing
  • 21 Americans Infected With Monkeypox, C.D.C. Reports
  • Axon Pauses Plans for Taser Drone as Ethics Board Members Resign
News from the New York Times

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 6 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-06 19:02PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Funding for Hong Kong child protection group cut by HK$1.8 million after abuse allegations
  • Hong Kong’s top officials could be put under week-long ‘closed-loop’ arrangement ahead of 25th handover anniversary celebrations
  • Former Hong Kong Bar Association chairman tipped to become justice minister in John Lee’s governing team, with incumbent Teresa Cheng on the way out
  • Hong Kong’s economy to further improve if city’s Covid-19 situation remains under control, says finance chief
  • Beijing’s foreign ministry in Hong Kong hits back at Western consulates over messages marking Tiananmen Square crackdown anniversary
  • Alleged driver charged after online video shows car mounting safety island, travelling against flow of traffic in Hong Kong
  • Man slashed in knife attack in New Territories village, Hong Kong police search for suspects
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong health experts warn of rebound in cases, as city logs 543 new infections
  • LME faces Elliott Management’s US$456 million lawsuit for suspending nickel futures trading, cancelling trades
  • Wear masks over long term to reduce antibiotics use, University of Hong Kong researchers say
  • Can John Lee learn from Carrie Lam’s mistakes and rebuild public trust?
  • How the economic slowdown and zero-Covid threaten China’s global ambitions
  • China accuses Canada of stepping up reconnaissance against Chinese military
  • Coronavirus: Chinese tourists trickle back during Dragon Boat Festival, but far short of pre-pandemic levels
  • Shanghai reopening: Tesla’s Chinese buyers face up to 24 weeks for delivery as carmaker scrambles to catch up with demand in ‘China’s Motown’
  • Covid infections lower in people with food allergies – and not higher in asthmatics, study finds
  • Covid outbreak shuts down Chinese city as Beijing and Shanghai return to new normal
  • Taiwanese military set to get first set of locally made short-range drones as PLA threats intensify
  • With China’s big political reshuffle looming, new No 2 propaganda chief is appointed from party academy
  • June date set for China-sponsored Horn of Africa peace conference
  • Chinese debt traps in Africa? The bigger worry is bondholders, study finds
  • China services activity contracts for third straight month in May as coronavirus ‘still weighs heavy’
  • Tech war: Chinese database software vendor shrugs off sanctions risk on using open-source code from Oracle’s MySQL system
  • Coronavirus: how the Shanghai outbreak exposed the Chinese health system’s fragility
  • Sri Lanka’s Rajapaksa to finish term despite protests: ‘I can’t go as failed president’
  • Indian products pulled by Kuwait supermarket in row over Prophet remarks
  • Australia’s Albanese, Indonesia’s Jokowi ride bamboo bikes, talk ‘green’ investment, trade and language support
  • At least 26 dead in India’s northern state after bus falls into gorge
  • Indonesia woos digital nomads with 5-year remote working visa, and promotes spiritual getaways, to bring in longer-staying visitors
  • Lawrence Wong becomes Singapore’s No 2 leader, cementing position as Lee Hsien Loong’s successor
  • In Australia, one man is on the hunt for the world’s most dangerous spider
  • Coronavirus: Singapore should prepare for a new Covid-19 wave in July or August, health minister says
  • Malaysia palm group warns of losses ahead from ‘severe’ labour crunch
  • ‘Nothing less than hate speech’: Google ordered to pay Australian politician US$515,000 over defamatory YouTube videos
  • Jack Ma’s Ant Group unveils Singapore digital bank – ANEXT – in overseas expansion push
  • Australia-China relations: Albanese reaches out to Beijing over fighter jet encounter
  • How rising India’s Quad role is helping it find its place among global powers
  • US, South Korea launch eight missiles in response to North Korea missile firings
  • Top Gun heirs sue Paramount over Maverick sequel
  • Ukraine war: US orders two Roman Abramovich jets seized over Russia sanctions
  • Mass shootings now define American exceptionalism
  • US suspends tariffs on some solar panel imports for two years, but leaves China out
  • Ukraine recovers steel plant siege bodies from last-ditch stand in Mariupol
  • South Africans visiting UK on Ryanair quizzed in Afrikaans to prove identity
  • ‘New Kazakhstan’ backs sweeping reforms to end strongman Nazarbayev’s era
  • Some people still haven’t caught Covid-19. Why?
  • US must course-correct on gun violence after Texas school massacre to save itself
  • UK to give Ukraine long-range M270 rocket systems after Putin warnings
  • Russian general reportedly killed in eastern Ukraine, another high-ranking casualty
  • Russian foreign minister Lavrov’s trip to Serbia cancelled as neighbours close airspace
  • Ukraine’s President Zelensky visits close to war front line near Sievierodonetsk
  • Joe Biden scrambles to avoid Americas Summit flop as boycott clouds US plans
  • UK’s PM Boris Johnson addresses his party’s lawmakers before confidence vote
  • Can Joe Biden convince the US’ Middle East allies he still has their back?
  • Ukraine claims its forces control ‘half’ of Sievierodonetsk against Russia
  • UK government accused of trying to deport unaccompanied minors to Rwanda
  • Gulf states condemn Indian official’s ‘Islamophobic’ remarks about the Prophet Mohammed
  • Ethiopia accuses aid agencies of delivering banned equipment to Tigray that could be used by rebels
  • Elon Musk says Twitter’s lack of information on bots breaches terms of their merger deal
  • China’s box office receipts hit 10-year low as ongoing Covid-19 restrictions produce a sad storyline for country’s cinemas
  • Didi to resume app downloads and new user registrations as Beijing security probe nears completion: report
  • Banks, technology giants see growing number of skilled employees leaving for flexible fintech firms, new analysis shows
  • Fiscal spending a better tool than monetary easing to boost China’s economic growth
  • BYD, Meituan fuel tech surge in Hong Kong as Beijing rolls back Covid-19 curbs, HKEX unrattled by LME lawsuit
  • Ant Group launches digital banking service in Singapore to serve small firms doing international trades
  • Hong Kong should stick to four-decade-old US dollar currency peg as it braces for ‘unusual challenges’ ahead, HKMA former CEO Yam says
  • Europe’s top money manager Amundi lends weight to Chinese stocks as market looks for sustained rebound
  • Like all wars, the US tech war on China is proving costly and pointless
  • Amazon cloud’s latest collaboration with Hong Kong science park seen as vote of confidence in city’s role as regional tech hub
  • Developing tomorrow’s tech talents can fuel Hong Kong’s ambitions to become the next Silicon Valley
  • China gaming ban: no new licenses in May points to ‘new normal’ in regulatory hostility
  • Why depression in old age is different, and how to deal with it – in yourself or a loved one
  • Irma Vep, HBO drama series starring Alicia Vikander, sees director Olivier Assayas revisit theme of his 1996 film with fascinating results
  • Myanmar artist Aung Myint’s evolving style on show in Hong Kong exhibition
  • 2 unique places to visit in Malaysia but which of Penang’s Blue and Green Mansions is best? We compare tours, price and overall experience
  • Ancient Chinese porn served as sex education and was even used for fire prevention
  • Louis Vuitton, Rolex and Tag Heuer at 2022 Monaco Grand Prix highlight how luxury brands and sports have never been closer
  • Who is the father of Amber Heard’s baby? Meet daughter Oonagh Paige: during the Johnny Depp case, the actress spoke out about being a mum, with plenty of fans wondering if Elon Musk is the baby daddy
  • How Madonna’s son David Banda is following in her footsteps: the 16 year old from Malawi made headlines by wearing an Adidas x Gucci dress as tribute to his famous mum – and he sings and dances too
  • 5 most exciting films premiering in June, from the third Jurassic World movie Dominion, to Pixar’s Lightyear and Netflix sci-fi feature Spiderhead starring Chris Hemsworth
  • The 13 best Japanese restaurants in Hong Kong: Sushi Wadatsumi, Shikigiku Japanese Restaurant, Sushi Saito at Four Seasons Hong Kong, Ryota Kappou Modern and Zuicho are all leading the wave
  • How the high jewellery market boomed in Asia during Covid-19: Chanel, Cartier, Bulgari, Van Cleef & Arpels and Graff used Zoom and private viewings for clients to maintain relationships
  • 7 Indian LGBT icons you need to know: from YouTuber Lilly Singh, Olympian Dutee Chand and gay royal Prince Manvendra Singh Gohil, to transgender activist Laxmi Narayan Tripathi and film director Onir
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Mickelson added to field for inaugural LIV Golf event
  • Myanmar junta slams France over 'illegitimate' snub, threatens ties
  • India says new social media laws in response to rights violations

entry.title: Guardian: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Sievierodonetsk and Lysychansk are ‘dead cities’, says Zelenskiy; Russian ships pushed back, Ukraine claims – live
  • Apple unveils buy now, pay later feature and redesigned iPhone lock screen
  • Capitol attack panel found evidence of ‘concerted planning and premeditated activity’, says congressman – live
  • Boris Johnson no-confidence vote under way as Tory MPs cast ballots to decide prime minister’s future – live
  • Croatia v France, Austria v Denmark: Nations League – live!
  • Dominican Republic environment minister shot dead in his office
  • Threat to bin Twitter deal over bots makes Elon Musk look like a time-waster
  • Women’s Tour of Britain: Copponi wins first stage after delay and late crash
  • Russia to pay £65,000 to families of national guards killed in Ukraine or Syria
  • Chinese star taken offline after showing ‘tank cake’ on Tiananmen anniversary
  • Hedley frontman Jacob Hoggard convicted of violent rape
  • Ukraine needs many more rocket launchers from west, says adviser
  • Briton given 15 years in Iraqi jail for smuggling antiquities to appeal verdict
  • Fears for safety of British journalist missing in Brazilian Amazon
  • This horrifying play makes people faint. How do the actors stay sane, night after night?
  • ‘A dangerous act’: how a Chinese fighter jet intercepted an RAAF aircraft and what happens next
  • Is this turmeric an eternal stain? How to remove the toughest spots – from ink to sunscreen
  • Stuart Broad: it would be pathetic to fall out with Joe Root over Windies snub
  • Boris Johnson makes late appeal to Tory MPs before confidence vote
  • Nearly half of Republicans think US has to live with mass shootings, poll finds
  • Boris Johnson’s highs and lows: from party hero to Partygate
  • Carbon dioxide levels are now 50% higher than during the pre-industrial era
  • Studio behind Tom Cruise blockbuster Top Gun: Maverick sued over copyright
  • Emma Raducanu reflects on ‘pretty surreal’ year before Nottingham return
  • Here’s the result of tonight’s no-confidence vote: a huge lurch to the right and more austerity | Owen Jones
  • Taser abandons plans to build stun gun-equipped drones for schools
  • Bored, cross, cheeky: how Prince Louis captured the mood of the nation
  • ‘Crazy journey’: how Wales went from worse than Haiti to World Cup heroes | Ben Fisher
  • John Oliver on cops in schools: ‘Criminalizes the very essence of childhood’
  • Wales’s glorious march to the World Cup is symbolic of a confident, resurgent nation | Darren Chetty
  • Joyous Nadal eases clear of Djokovic and Federer in race to be the greatest | Tumaini Carayol
  • Inquiry opens into alleged patient abuse at Muckamore Abbey
  • Have you got Lesbian Fighting Song? The Pride anthems that time forgot
  • ‘It drove New York crazy’ – Arthur Baker and Freeez on making club classic IOU
  • Elon Musk threatens to scrap $44bn Twitter deal over ‘material breach’
  • The Silent Treatment: Sarah-Louise Young on the trauma and stigma of losing her voice
  • Archive film shows Belfast kids going wild to Hippy Hippy Shake in 1964
  • Pope Francis’s latest plans fuel rumours over resignation
  • Anticyclone Scipio turns up heat in Mediterranean and north Africa
  • Of course French police and politicians lied and smeared UK football fans. That’s what they do | Fabrice Arfi
  • James Milner signs one-year contract to stay at Liverpool on reduced wages
  • European unity on Ukraine growing more difficult, says Estonian PM
  • I’ll raise a glass to the no-confidence vote – it is prolonging Britain’s sense of togetherness | Zoe Williams
  • Paris protesters celebrate saving trees around the Eiffel Tower
  • US navy pilot killed in fighter-jet crash in California desert
  • Lou Reed’s earliest Velvet Underground demos unearthed for reissue
  • France puts 18 on trial over alleged involvement in vast horsemeat scandal
  • Energy prices and Ukraine war no excuse for climate inaction, say experts
  • The big idea: is a world without violent conflict really possible?
  • The people making a difference: she was devastated by vaginal-mesh surgery. Now she helps others recover
  • Writing honestly about motherhood still provokes anger, but we must tell our stories | Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett
  • ‘The occupier should never feel safe’: rise in partisan attacks in Ukraine
  • Ryanair forces South Africans to do Afrikaans test to prove nationality
  • ‘I had to tape my fingers together to stop me tearing the skin’: the reality of living with Tourette syndrome
  • Prophet Muhammad remarks embroil India in row with Gulf states
  • Environmentalists join forces to fight ‘carbon bomb’ fossil fuel projects
  • Family ties a poor excuse for fading players signing up to Saudi golf riches | Barry Glendenning
  • Martin Scorsese on Ray Liotta in Goodfellas: ‘The new guy never missed a beat’
  • ‘Our country is getting old’: the man changing how Brazil sees dementia
  • Legendary hit-maker Linda Perry: ‘Singers have to earn my songs. I don’t just give them out’
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: Financial Times: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Musk threatens to abandon $44bn Twitter takeover
  • How ESG investing came to a reckoning

  • Investors back business-focused buy now, pay later groups
  • Canadian pension fund makes $2.5bn bet on Dubai Ports

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
